mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to decreased consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for housing, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to decreased economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real personal consumption expenditures signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decrease in the federal funds rate and a sustained increase in the consumer price index signal a monetary policy mistake, leading to increased inflation expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the unemployment rate and a sharp increase in payroll employment signal a strengthening labor market, leading to increased economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
